hook.text: Every feature you build has a hidden cost: the features you didn't build instead. This is opportunity cost.

buildup.text: A team building a dashboard for three months can't build the onboarding improvement that might double activation.

discovery.text: Opportunity cost makes prioritization a zero-sum game. Choosing one thing means unchoosing everything else for that period.

twist.text: Teams rarely calculate opportunity cost explicitly. They evaluate ideas in isolation instead of asking 'what are we giving up?'

climax.text: The best PMs don't just ask 'is this worth building?' They ask 'is this worth building instead of everything else we could build?'

punchline.text: The cost of building the wrong thing is everything else you didn't build.

quiz.question: Why is opportunity cost important in product prioritization?
options: Every feature built means other potentially better features aren't built
Opportunity cost only applies to financial decisions
Building more features eliminates opportunity cost
